schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: e3939ee1e3fb7c7cabac8cf75b7a540585caebf2
findings:
  - { id: judged-f-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2's pre-filled frozen stamp is in undeclared tension with parent dc-2's fabrication prohibition ('a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record'). The frozen.commit field names the tree the claim was verified against — that is part of the human oracle's record, not mere structure — yet the helper writes HEAD-at-scaffold-time before any verification has occurred. The story's own mitigation (ADJ-30: operator updates frozen.commit when authoring; instructional prose says so) is procedural, not mechanical: the dc-3 unauthored sentinel guards only the claim body, so an operator who authors the claim but forgets to update frozen.commit commits a machine-fabricated verification target that VL-010 then binds as frozen record. The story flags this for seam review and cites ADJ-30, but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-2 is declared; if ADJ-30's fold-in does not constitute such an edge, this is an undeclared conflict. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "ADJ-30 adjudicated this exact tension (cf-1, confidence 0.60) and accepted it WITH REFINEMENT, now folded into dc-2 (frontmatter and body), ac-1, and dc-3: the pre-filled frozen stamp is a convenience matching the store's proven attestation convention. An attestation's frozen.commit names the tree the claim was verified against, never the file's own commit (witness: attestation/disclosure-legibility--ac-1 stamps 6a3465b, a commit predating that file's own existence). Post-refinement there is no conflict with parent dc-2: the machine writes structure and pre-fills a starting point, while the operator owns the epistemic half (which tree was verified) and commits it. Committing the stamp is the human vouching for it, the identical trust posture a hand-authored frozen.commit has always carried, not machine fabrication. The residual 'operator authors the claim but forgets to update frozen.commit' case is covered by the same ruling: the stamp is legally mutable until first commit and VL-010 binds only committed frozen artifacts, so whatever value the human commits is the value the human vouched for. Resolving a judged tension through this disposition layer (rather than a supersedes/exempts edge, which is the computed half's device) is the designed path. Enum mapping, disclosed: ADJ-30's accepted-with-refinement has no literal ConflictDisposition value, so no-conflict is the faithful carrier (the finding correctly prompted the clarification and after it there is no genuine conflict), mirroring the illustrative-class precedent that dispositions a structurally identical 0.60 'no supersedes/exempts edge declared' finding as no-conflict. See ADJ-30.", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-f-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi attest) for ac-2 while parent dc-5 rejected adding any new verb for ac-1 on the rationale that 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal.' Attestation scaffolding is part of the same closure ritual dc-5 concentrated in verdi close, and dc-1's rejected-alternatives analysis considered board but never a close-mode, so dc-5's one-verb-owns-the-ritual rationale is bypassed without a declared edge. Likely benign — dc-5 explicitly scoped its no-new-verb ruling to ac-1 and dc-1 discloses the 3.R ratification dependency — but the boundary between dc-5's rationale and dc-1's choice is undeclared. (confidence 0.15)", disposition: rejected, note: "ADJ-30 rejected cf-2 (this finding restated, confidence 0.20 then 0.15) on the same grounds as ADJ-29 f-2: parent dc-5's verb-economy clause is explicitly ac-1-scoped ('No new verb is added for ac-1'), and parent ac-2 speaks of 'a helper verb' in terms, so dc-5's one-verb-owns-the-ritual rationale does not reach ac-2. The new top-level verb is deliberate scoping, not an undeclared conflict, and dc-1 discloses the 3.R ratification dependency it requires; the finding itself concedes it is likely benign. Enum mapping, disclosed: ADJ-30's rejected maps to the ConflictDisposition value rejected. See ADJ-30.", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/attest-helper#dc-1, spec/attest-helper#dc-2, spec/attest-helper#dc-3, spec/attest-helper#dc-4, spec/attest-helper#dc-5, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-1, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-2, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-3, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-4, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:8d1b30f109a0aff83d1984e7ac74f261beac2df99e2d979a1b771dc24fb72b0e
integrity: sha256:9ce77079b733ce335e77e895e85d7582c98c6824840f92cbb65ef0b9ba600aeb
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f-1\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2's pre-filled frozen stamp is in undeclared tension with parent dc-2's fabrication prohibition ('a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record'). The frozen.commit field names the tree the claim was verified against — that is part of the human oracle's record, not mere structure — yet the helper writes HEAD-at-scaffold-time before any verification has occurred. The story's own mitigation (ADJ-30: operator updates frozen.commit when authoring; instructional prose says so) is procedural, not mechanical: the dc-3 unauthored sentinel guards only the claim body, so an operator who authors the claim but forgets to update frozen.commit commits a machine-fabricated verification target that VL-010 then binds as frozen record. The story flags this for seam review and cites ADJ-30, but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-2 is declared; if ADJ-30's fold-in does not constitute such an edge, this is an undeclared conflict.\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"f-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi attest) for ac-2 while parent dc-5 rejected adding any new verb for ac-1 on the rationale that 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal.' Attestation scaffolding is part of the same closure ritual dc-5 concentrated in verdi close, and dc-1's rejected-alternatives analysis considered board but never a close-mode, so dc-5's one-verb-owns-the-ritual rationale is bypassed without a declared edge. Likely benign — dc-5 explicitly scoped its no-new-verb ruling to ac-1 and dc-1 discloses the 3.R ratification dependency — but the boundary between dc-5's rationale and dc-1's choice is undeclared.\",\"confidence\":0.15,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/attest-helper@e3939ee1e3fb7c7cabac8cf75b7a540585caebf2], digest: sha256:8d1b30f109a0aff83d1984e7ac74f261beac2df99e2d979a1b771dc24fb72b0e, integrity: sha256:9ce77079b733ce335e77e895e85d7582c98c6824840f92cbb65ef0b9ba600aeb }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-f-1** [no-conflict]: Story dc-2's pre-filled frozen stamp is in undeclared tension with parent dc-2's fabrication prohibition ('a machine must not manufacture a human oracle's record'). The frozen.commit field names the tree the claim was verified against — that is part of the human oracle's record, not mere structure — yet the helper writes HEAD-at-scaffold-time before any verification has occurred. The story's own mitigation (ADJ-30: operator updates frozen.commit when authoring; instructional prose says so) is procedural, not mechanical: the dc-3 unauthored sentinel guards only the claim body, so an operator who authors the claim but forgets to update frozen.commit commits a machine-fabricated verification target that VL-010 then binds as frozen record. The story flags this for seam review and cites ADJ-30, but no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-2 is declared; if ADJ-30's fold-in does not constitute such an edge, this is an undeclared conflict. (confidence 0.35) — ADJ-30 adjudicated this exact tension (cf-1, confidence 0.60) and accepted it WITH REFINEMENT, now folded into dc-2 (frontmatter and body), ac-1, and dc-3: the pre-filled frozen stamp is a convenience matching the store's proven attestation convention. An attestation's frozen.commit names the tree the claim was verified against, never the file's own commit (witness: attestation/disclosure-legibility--ac-1 stamps 6a3465b, a commit predating that file's own existence). Post-refinement there is no conflict with parent dc-2: the machine writes structure and pre-fills a starting point, while the operator owns the epistemic half (which tree was verified) and commits it. Committing the stamp is the human vouching for it, the identical trust posture a hand-authored frozen.commit has always carried, not machine fabrication. The residual 'operator authors the claim but forgets to update frozen.commit' case is covered by the same ruling: the stamp is legally mutable until first commit and VL-010 binds only committed frozen artifacts, so whatever value the human commits is the value the human vouched for. Resolving a judged tension through this disposition layer (rather than a supersedes/exempts edge, which is the computed half's device) is the designed path. Enum mapping, disclosed: ADJ-30's accepted-with-refinement has no literal ConflictDisposition value, so no-conflict is the faithful carrier (the finding correctly prompted the clarification and after it there is no genuine conflict), mirroring the illustrative-class precedent that dispositions a structurally identical 0.60 'no supersedes/exempts edge declared' finding as no-conflict. See ADJ-30.
- **judged-f-2** [rejected]: Story dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi attest) for ac-2 while parent dc-5 rejected adding any new verb for ac-1 on the rationale that 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal.' Attestation scaffolding is part of the same closure ritual dc-5 concentrated in verdi close, and dc-1's rejected-alternatives analysis considered board but never a close-mode, so dc-5's one-verb-owns-the-ritual rationale is bypassed without a declared edge. Likely benign — dc-5 explicitly scoped its no-new-verb ruling to ac-1 and dc-1 discloses the 3.R ratification dependency — but the boundary between dc-5's rationale and dc-1's choice is undeclared. (confidence 0.15) — ADJ-30 rejected cf-2 (this finding restated, confidence 0.20 then 0.15) on the same grounds as ADJ-29 f-2: parent dc-5's verb-economy clause is explicitly ac-1-scoped ('No new verb is added for ac-1'), and parent ac-2 speaks of 'a helper verb' in terms, so dc-5's one-verb-owns-the-ritual rationale does not reach ac-2. The new top-level verb is deliberate scoping, not an undeclared conflict, and dc-1 discloses the 3.R ratification dependency it requires; the finding itself concedes it is likely benign. Enum mapping, disclosed: ADJ-30's rejected maps to the ConflictDisposition value rejected. See ADJ-30.
